retrieved_docs.content: Altered passive eruption ("gummy smile") with bone approximating or covering the cementoenamel junction, resulting in an excess gingival display; no restoration is needed

retrieved_docs.title: Stainless Steel Crowns in Primary Dentition -- Technique or Treatment -- Crown Adaptation
retrieved_docs.content: Operators can use the number 137 pliers can be used for circumferential contouring. Contouring pliers deliver a belling effect in the middle third of the crown. [19] Johnson contouring pliers with a No. 114 are used for cervical crown adaptation on the cervical third of the buccal and lingual surfaces. A curved beak plier No. 114 is then used to contour the buccal and lingual surfaces. Curved beak pliers may also contour the crown's proximal areas. [20] [13] Lastly, the crimping pliers are used to crimp the cervical margin 1 mm circumferentially for final adaptation and ensure that the crown margin's outline follows the gingival margin of the tooth. The operators can check the final adaptation of the crown by taking a radiograph to verify the gingival contour and extension, ensuring the complete coverage of the tooth. [13] [21]

retrieved_docs.title: Stainless Steel Crowns in Primary Dentition -- Technique or Treatment -- Crown Adaptation
retrieved_docs.content: Crown adaptation is critical for retention and gum protection. A poorly adapted crown is a source of plaque and bacterial accumulation, resulting in gingivitis and recurrent cervical caries. Spedding advocated 2 key principles to adjust a stainless steel crown correctly. The operator must determine the appropriate occlusal-gingival crown length. Crown margins should be contoured circumferentially to follow the natural contours of the marginal gingiva of the tooth. Steps

retrieved_docs.title: Glass Ionomer Cement -- Function -- Indications
retrieved_docs.content: Restorative material: Thanks to its easy placement and better marginal adaptation, glass ionomer cement is popularly used in pediatric restorations. GIC is also indicated for restoring permanent teeth in low stress-bearing areas like class III and V lesions and is the material of choice in patients with a high risk of caries due to the release of fluoride. Luting agent: GIC can lute indirect restorations (metal and metal-ceramic), post, core, and orthodontic bands, and brackets. [4] [8] Pulp protection: GIC is used as a liner or base beneath metallic and composite restorations (sandwich technique). [9]

retrieved_docs.title: Stainless Steel Crowns in Primary Dentition -- Technique or Treatment -- Cementation
retrieved_docs.content: A luting cement should be used to secure the crown. Once the crown is seated on the prepared tooth, it should be kept under pressure while the cement hardens. Manufacturers recommend glass ionomer, zinc polycarboxylate, and zinc phosphate cement but fluoride-leaching cement has additional advantages. Due to the clinical advantages, RMGIC is the preferred choice. [22] However, research suggests the type of cement used has little effect on retention, with the essential retentive components derived from proper crown contouring and crimping. Operators must pay deliberate attention to the removal of excess cement. This is normally accomplished by running a pointed instrument around the cemented crown's margins and sliding knotted dental floss buccolingually through the contact areas after the cement setting. Excess cement has been shown to affect gingival health. [8] [6]

retrieved_docs.title: Stainless Steel Crowns in Primary Dentition -- Technique or Treatment -- Buccal/Lingual Reduction
retrieved_docs.content: Reducing the buccal and lingual surfaces is the third step in the preparation. The reduction of the buccal and lingual mucosa is optional. Some authors suggested preparing the buccal and lingual walls to create a gingivally inclined long bevel to make crown placement easier. On the other hand, others advocated for none to minimal (0.5 to 1 mm) preparation on the vestibular and lingual aspect of the crown, unless pronounced enamel convexity should only be reduced by a small amount. [12] Duggal and Curzon recommended trying the selected crown before lingual and buccal reduction. [13] Lastly, the operator should round all line and point angles. Occlusal-lingual and occlusal-buccal angles are beveled at 45 degrees. Bur is held parallel to the tooth’s long axis, and one should carefully blend the surfaces. Remove any remaining caries and perform appropriate pulp therapy if pulp involvement is present. The rubber dam should be left in place until cementing the crown. If it interferes with the seating and adaptation of the crown, it can be removed after tooth preparation. [6]

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 3 -- Level 2 -- Community 1198

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 2 -- Level 3 -- Community 2117
retrieved_docs.content: assessment of complication rates and effectiveness. Trend qualifiers capture evolving usage patterns, technological advances, and guideline updates for all listed interventions, ensuring that clinical decision‑making remains aligned with current best practices. The community also uses an “Allowed” qualifier to indicate that quantitative statistical and numerical data are collected and reported for each of these procedures, and a parallel “trendy” qualifier to designate them as emerging, widely discussed, or of heightened clinical interest. By linking statistical metrics with trend status, the framework supports clinicians, researchers, and health administrators in monitoring performance indicators, evaluating evolving practice patterns, and prioritizing resource allocation for procedures that are both data‑rich and gaining prominence in patient care.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 33 -- Level 1 -- Community 109
retrieved_docs.content: disorders, and obstetric interventions together form a comprehensive framework for risk assessment, care planning, and management of maternal‑fetal outcomes during pregnancy, delivery, and the postpartum period.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 0 -- Level 4 -- Community 2912
retrieved_docs.content: Pregnancy-Related Procedures is a broad category of obstetric interventions that encompasses specific techniques such as بضع الفرج and Vaginal Assisted Delivery, each classified as a child procedure within this group. Both بضع الفرج and Vaginal Assisted Delivery represent assisted vaginal delivery methods used to facilitate safe childbirth when spontaneous vaginal delivery is not optimal, highlighting the importance of tailored procedural options in maternal care.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 0 -- Level 4 -- Community 2791
retrieved_docs.content: Episiotomy and تمزق الأغشية الاصطناعي (artificial membrane rupture) are both classified under الإجراءات العلاجية الخاصة بالتوليد (obstetric therapeutic procedures). By recognizing these interventions as subcomponents of the broader category of obstetric therapeutic care, clinicians can better organize treatment options, understand their related indications, and apply appropriate management strategies within the field of obstetrics.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 28 -- Level 3 -- Community 1697
retrieved_docs.content: A caesarean section scar of unknown type and a poor reproductive history are clinically associated with مخاض قبل الأوان (preterm labor), indicating that women who have an indeterminate type of previous cesarean scar or who have experienced suboptimal reproductive outcomes are at increased risk for premature labor. Recognizing these associations enables clinicians to identify high‑risk patients, implement closer surveillance, and consider preventive strategies to reduce the likelihood of preterm delivery.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 28 -- Level 2 -- Community 551

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 3 -- Level 3 -- Community 1702
retrieved_docs.content: CAESAREAN SECTION SCAR TYPE UNKNOWN and High risk are both clinically associated with cesarean section, indicating that an unidentified type of cesarean scar and a high‑risk patient status are relevant factors in the consideration, planning, and postoperative management of cesarean deliveries.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 7 -- Level 4 -- Community 2769
retrieved_docs.content: Low transverse cesarean section is clinically associated with تمزق الأغشية الباكر (premature rupture of membranes), indicating that this surgical approach to delivery may be linked to an increased occurrence of early membrane rupture, a factor that clinicians must consider when planning obstetric care and managing potential complications.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 6 -- Level 3 -- Community 1895
retrieved_docs.content: Removal of cerclage from cervix is a medical intervention that is directly related to the cervical cerclage procedure (إجراء تطويق عنق الرحم). This step involves extracting the sutures that were placed around the cervix to reinforce cervical competence and prevent premature dilation, and it represents a specific phase within the overall management of cervical insufficiency. Recognizing the connection between the removal of the cerclage and the broader cerclage procedure is essential for appropriate patient care and timing of intervention.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 0 -- Level 1 -- Community 209
retrieved_docs.content: constitute major complications of pregnancy, childbirth, and the puerperium. Recognizing the placement of each specific condition within its broader category supports comprehensive assessment, risk stratification, and coordinated management of pregnant patients.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 34 -- Level 2 -- Community 1021
retrieved_docs.content: organizes general obstetric procedure categories (إجراء توليدي, Antepartum Procedures, Obstetrics incision) and specific clinical actions—ranging from diagnostic sampling and membrane management to fetal stimulation, reduction of multiple gestations, and surgical treatment of ectopic pregnancies—providing a comprehensive framework for coordinating obstetric care across the ante‑partum, intrapartum, and postpartum periods.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 2 -- Level 3 -- Community 2039
retrieved_docs.content: Maternal care for other type of scar from previous cesarean delivery is classified as عملية قيصرية سابقة, indicating that this category of maternal care pertains to women who have a scar from a prior cesarean section. This classification helps healthcare providers identify and manage patients with previous cesarean delivery scars within maternal‑care protocols.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 8 -- Level 5 -- Community 2926
retrieved_docs.content: Cesarean section is clinically associated with the low transverse cesarean section technique, and the low transverse cesarean section is in turn clinically associated with early membrane rupture (تمزق الأغشية الباكر). This relationship highlights that the specific low transverse incision used during a cesarean delivery may be linked to an increased likelihood of premature rupture of the fetal membranes, a factor that clinicians consider when planning surgical approaches and managing obstetric risk.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 19 -- Level 2 -- Community 1017
retrieved_docs.content: a wide range of obstetric actions—from diagnostic punctures and biopsies to major surgical operations and fetal‑positioning techniques—under the unified concept of إجراءات توليدي, facilitating clearer clinical categorization, planning, and patient‑care coordination. The postpartum obstetric operation is closely linked to the provision of immediate postpartum care and investigation, emphasizing the importance of timely assessment and management following delivery.
